calendar.matters:
Motion For Reclassification Of Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MARGARITA LOPEZ DE BOXERBAUM's motion to reclassify this action from a limited civil action to an unlimited civil action is granted. The motion must be granted if two conditions are satisfied: (1) the case is incorrectly classified and (b) the moving party shows good cause for not seeking reclassification earlier. (Code Civ. Proc., 403.040, subd. (b).) Plaintiff meets both conditions. The trial court is required to determine whether, based on the record before the court, a judgment in excess of $25,000 is obtainable. (Ytuarte v. Superior Court (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 266, 279.) Plaintiff is not required to show with a "high level of certainty" that the damage award will exceed $25,000 to obtain reclassification. (Id.) Instead, plaintiff must present evidence to demonstrate a "possibility" the damages will exceed $25,000. (Id.) Here, this case is incorrectly classified as a "limited" matter because the Medicare lien is $70,765.09, which easily meets the standard. Plaintiff demonstrates good cause for not seeking reclassification earlier as Plaintiff did not receive or know about the $70,765.09 Medicare lien until May 20, 2019. (Chan Decl. Ex. C.) When Plaintiff filed the complaint on June 20, 2018, counsel knew Plaintiff's medical bills were being paid by either Medi-Cal or Medicare and filed the matter as a limited civil action believing that Medi-Cal/Medicare would be reimbursing medical providers at a much lower rate. (Chan Decl. 5.) Until the receipt of the Medicare lien, Plaintiff had no reason to amend the complaint to reclassify or file a motion to reclassify this matter from limited to unlimited. (Chan Decl. 20.) The City will not be prejudiced by the reclassification, as the complaint was not served until January 11, 2019, and Plaintiff's deposition has not yet been taken. Plaintiff shall pay the reclassification fee within 10 days of entry of the court's order. (See Code Civ. Proc., 403.040 subd. (c)(1).). Order signed. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported (302/EPS)
